title: Market Brief — Monday, May 11, 2026
summary: GPU compute is tightening hard across next-gen silicon: B200 availability at 25% signals scarcity, GB200 at 10% is a severe shortage, and H200 at 45% is heading the same direction, while legacy A100 sits in oversupply at 85% and MI300X is comfortable at 60%. The biggest price signal is H200 OCPI at $4.25/hr with 87.5% annualized volatility and utilization surging 18.5 points to 95.1% in 30 days, confirming a demand squeeze on bleeding-edge inference hardware.
paragraphs: GPU compute is tightening hard across next-gen silicon: B200 availability at 25% signals scarcity, GB200 at 10% is a severe shortage, and H200 at 45% is heading the same direction, while legacy A100 sits in oversupply at 85% and MI300X is comfortable at 60%. The biggest price signal is H200 OCPI at $4.25/hr with 87.5% annualized volatility and utilization surging 18.5 points to 95.1% in 30 days, confirming a demand squeeze on bleeding-edge inference hardware.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), Llama 70B inference runs roughly $9.48/M on A100, $9.84/M on H100, and $11.90/M on MI300X, meaning any provider pricing medium-tier output at $3/M is burning roughly $6-9 per million tokens. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.378/M output and Meta Llama 4 Maverick at $0.60/M are selling at catastrophic losses against self-hosted TCO. Provider grades: OpenAI Neutral, Anthropic Strong, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

B200 and GB200 compute is undervalued given scarcity, surging utilization, and the inference cost advantage B200 holds at $5.03/M TCO-adjusted versus $9.84/M on H100. A100 capacity is overvalued with flat utilization at 79% and oversupply at 85% availability, so rotate out of legacy GPU exposure and lock in B200 forward capacity before availability drops below 15%.
bullets: 10% GB200 availability marks severe shortage, the tightest supply of any GPU class tracked.
25% B200 availability qualifies as scarce while utilization jumped 23.5 percentage points in 30 days.
$0.378/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 implies a per-token loss exceeding $11/M against TCO-adjusted self-hosted inference.
21.41 LLMflation index means frontier token prices have fallen roughly 79% since GPT-4 launch.
87.5% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI is the highest across all GPU classes.
$80/M output pricing on o3 Pro is 211x more expensive than DeepSeek V3.2 output.
